trip.name: Toronto → Texas → West Coast → Rockies → Home (35 days)
statsChips: One leg at a time — you’ve got this
trip.overviewLead: 35-day Canada ↔ USA motorcycle loop in three chapters: east → Texas home → west coast, Cascades, Yellowstone, and home through the plains. Includes a Finger Lakes → New York segment before DC and Luray.
planningChecklist: Confirm dates, host addresses, and camping fees before you roll.
Days marked TBD still need pins — use each day’s Google Maps link to lock distance.
Fill fuel / weather / key notes per day as you research (same idea as your Word plan).
Summer wildfire smoke can hit NorCal, PNW, Rockies, and high plains on short notice — check AQI/smoke maps for your corridor the morning you ride; shorten or reroute when air is unhealthy.
Every iOverlander / BLM / dispersed GPS pin: confirm land status, MVUM, and fire closures the same week you stay — crowdsourced pins are not permission to camp.
trip.summary: 35-day ride stringing together the Finger Lakes, a New York metro stop, Washington DC and Luray, southwest Virginia, Great Smoky approaches, Nashville–Memphis, Arkansas, a Fort Worth home stop, then the high plains and I-40 toward New Mexico. From there: San Juan Mountains and Million Dollar Highway, Utah (Mexican Hat, Page, Bryce, Moab with a full rest day at Arches, St. George), the California coast (Leo Carrillo day stop, Big Sur, redwoods), Oregon and the Columbia River Gorge, Seattle, US-20 and Spokane, Lolo Pass, Yellowstone and the Beartooth corridor, and a long return through the Dakotas and Great Lakes to Ontario, with a final night in London before Toronto.

days.title: Toronto → Finger Lakes (NY)
days.routeLine: Plan in your mapping app — typical: GTA → Niagara/Lewiston or Peace Bridge corridor → I-90 east into NY → Finger Lakes roads (verify crossing and tolls).
days.fuelNotes: Fill in Ontario before the border if convenient; I-90 NY has frequent fuel after entry.
days.foodNotes: Pack snacks for border queue; rural Finger Lakes — know dinner plan with hosts or town stop.
days.weatherNotes: Lake breeze and frontal weather near Ontario / Erie — check wind on bridge approaches.
keyNotes: Border: registration, insurance, declare tools/spares as needed; keep answers simple (touring).
Save offline maps for NY Thruway and last-mile to McGraw.

days.title: Albuquerque → Million Dollar Highway (Silverton, CO)

lodging.heading: Camping

lodging.notes: GPS 37.82022, -107.71362 — confirm MVUM / fire rules. Additional free dispersed camping along the route is possible — verify land status.

risks: Mountain chip seal and tar patches — traction drops fast when wet; treat painted lines and metal grates as low-grip.
days.planB: Paid campground or Silverton lodging if dispersed full or weather bad.

days.fuelNotes: Do not pass fuel under half tank; top off leaving Page or Kanab before climbing toward Bryce.
days.foodNotes: Page has services; carry water — long hot segments possible.
days.weatherNotes: Desert heat + isolated afternoon storms — start early; watch crosswinds on open mesas.
keyNotes: Antelope Canyon: guided tours sell out — book ahead.
Horseshoe Bend: paid parking, short hike, brutal midday sun.
End goal: confirm dispersed pin is still legal same week.

days.title: Moab — Arches & Dead Horse Point (Rest/Exploration Day)
days.routeLine: Zero riding day. Base: Moab, UT. Arches NP is 8 km from town — drive or ride out early before the heat and tourist crowds build.
days.fuelNotes: No fuel needed — day ride within Moab area only.
days.terrain: Paved park roads and short walking trails. No challenging riding.
highlights: Arches National Park — Delicate Arch hike (5 km round trip, start by 7 am)
Dead Horse Point State Park — 600 m canyon rim overlook above the Colorado River
Canyonlands NP Island in the Sky (optional 30-min drive from Dead Horse Point)
Moab town: gear shops, resupply, laundry, bike check

stops.label: Arches NP timed entry
stops.notes: Reserve timed entry on Recreation.gov — required May 1–Oct 31. Book as soon as possible.

stops.label: Dead Horse Point SP
stops.place: Dead Horse Point State Park, UT
stops.notes: 600 m above the Colorado River — one of the most photographed overlooks in the USA. Entry fee ~$20.

lodging.heading: Stay (Moab — second night)
lodging.name: Robert VandenBroeke
lodging.notes: Second consecutive night — confirm availability with host for 2-night stay.

lodgingAlternatives.heading: Camping (~$20/night)
lodgingAlternatives.name: Camp area (GPS)
lodgingAlternatives.notes: GPS 38.55729, -109.58466 — verify name, amenities, and booking.

lodgingAlternatives.heading: Motel option
lodgingAlternatives.name: Any Moab motel (book ahead)
lodgingAlternatives.notes: Moab fills fast in June — if host can only do 1 night, book motel for this night.

risks: Moab summer heat — Arches by 7 am, off trail by 11 am
Arches timed entry sells out weeks in advance — must pre-book
Dehydration risk on Delicate Arch hike — carry 2 L minimum
days.weatherNotes: June temperatures in Moab regularly exceed 38 °C (100 °F) by midday. All outdoor activity should be done before 10–11 am or after 5 pm.
days.planB: If Arches entry not available, Dead Horse Point + Canyonlands Island in the Sky is an equally spectacular full day.

days.title: St. George → Barstow (CA)
highlights: I-15 into California low desert

lodging.name: Montana Shaffer
lodging.notes: Apple Valley — near Barstow / Victorville; confirm ETA for late Mojave arrivals.

days.title: Barstow → Hollywood → Leo Carrillo (Day Stop) → Big Sur / Prewitt Ridge
days.routeLine: Barstow → I-15 S → CA-14 → LA basin → PCH (Hwy 1) north through Malibu → Leo Carrillo State Park (2-3 hr day stop) → PCH north through Big Sur → Prewitt Ridge Campground. ~590 km total — longest riding day of the California leg. Start by 6:30 am to clear LA traffic.
days.fuelNotes: Fuel in Barstow before departure. Next reliable fuel: Cambria or San Simeon (before Big Sur no-services stretch). Fill there.
days.terrain: CA freeways to coast — read current California DMV / CHP lane-splitting guidance; only allowed in specific situations. PCH curves, tar snakes, patch pavement, damp fog — reduced grip even when dry. Prewitt Ridge final climb: rough dirt road.
highlights: First sight of the Pacific Ocean after 17 days inland — emotional milestone for video
Leo Carrillo State Park (Malibu) — 2-3 hr stop: swim, beach content, food
PCH north through Ventura, Santa Barbara, and San Luis Obispo county
Bixby Creek Bridge — iconic Big Sur arch bridge photo stop
McWay Falls / Julia Pfeiffer Burns SP — short walk, waterfall into the ocean
Prewitt Ridge — BLM dispersed camp with Pacific view

stops.label: Day stop — Leo Carrillo
stops.place: Leo Carrillo State Park, Malibu, CA
stops.notes: 2-3 hour stop. Beach, swim, content creation — this is your Pacific arrival moment. No overnight here.

stops.label: Food / fuel resupply
stops.notes: Last reliable fuel before Big Sur. Fill tank and carry extra food — limited services on Hwy 1 north.

stops.label: Photo stop — Bixby Creek Bridge
stops.place: Bixby Creek Bridge, Big Sur, CA
stops.notes: Pull over on north side for the classic arch-over-coast shot.

lodging.name: Prewitt Ridge Campground
lodging.notes: Confirm road conditions for loaded DR650; carry 4 L water. Final climb is rough BLM dirt. Arrive before dark.

risks: LA traffic — depart Barstow by 6:30 am to beat morning rush
PCH weekend parking pressure at Malibu
Big Sur: Coastal Hwy 1 tar snakes, patch pavement, damp fog — reduced grip in corners
Prewitt Ridge: cliff exposure on approach road, private gate policies — verify access
Long day (~590 km) — monitor fatigue; Cambria is a bail-out overnight if needed
days.planB: If fatigued by Cambria: stay at state park camp (Hwy 1 corridor) and ride Prewitt Ridge the next morning before continuing north.

Heavy smoke: favor shorter riding, more breaks, and indoor air when AQI is unhealthy — especially at altitude.

highlights: Driftless optional detour if time

lodging.heading: Stay option
lodging.name: Jane Thompson
lodging.notes: Confirm address.

days.title: Oconomowoc → Benton Harbor (Day Stop, John Cody Smith) → London, ON
days.routeLine: Oconomowoc, WI → I-94 E → Benton Harbor, MI (visit John Cody Smith, 2 hr lunch stop) → US-12 or I-94 E → Port Huron, MI → Blue Water Bridge → Sarnia, ON → Hwy 402 E → London, ON. ~530 km total. Cross the border on Jul 4 afternoon — traffic flows INTO Canada on the American holiday, making it lighter than the July 5 return rush.
days.fuelNotes: Fuel in Oconomowoc before departure. Fill again at Port Huron before the border — Canadian fuel is priced in litres and typically higher. Cross with a full tank.
days.terrain: I-94 corridor — flat Midwest freeway. Blue Water Bridge (Port Huron–Sarnia): active CBSA crossing. Highway 402 to London: Ontario freeway, smooth.
highlights: John Cody Smith visit (Benton Harbor area) — 2 hr lunch + content stop
Lake Michigan shoreline approach on I-94
Blue Water Bridge at Port Huron/Sarnia — re-entering Canada
Canadian border crossing: note CBSA declaration requirements (duty-free limits after 48 hrs)
London, ON overnight — final Canadian city before Toronto

stops.label: Day stop — John Cody Smith
stops.place: Coloma, MI (near Benton Harbor / St. Joseph)
stops.notes: 2 hr lunch and content stop. Confirm arrival window with John. No overnight — continuing to London, ON same day.

stops.label: Border crossing
stops.place: Blue Water Bridge, Port Huron, MI → Sarnia, ON
stops.notes: Jul 4 afternoon crossing into Canada is lighter than Jul 5. Have passport/NEXUS ready. Declare all items accurately to CBSA.

lodging.name: TBD — book London, ON
lodging.notes: Any central London motel — book in advance. Last night before home leg tomorrow.

risks: Long day (~530 km) on US Independence Day — US-side traffic heavy mid-day; plan Benton Harbor stop early (11 am–1 pm) and border crossing by 3–4 pm
Blue Water Bridge border wait — check CBSA wait times that morning; have all documents ready
Michigan I-94 Jul 4 holiday traffic — allow extra time around Chicago-to-Detroit corridor
Canadian CBSA: strict about alcohol, firearms, and food items — declare everything
days.weatherNotes: July 4 in the Great Lakes region: typically warm (25–32 °C), possible afternoon thunderstorms. Check radar before departure.
days.planB: If John Cody Smith visit runs long or traffic is severe, skip London and stay in Sarnia/Windsor area instead.

days.title: Return home (London, ON → Toronto)
highlights: Final leg home

lodging.name: HOME — Toronto, ON
lodging.notes: Final leg from London — debrief, service bike, archive receipts.

documents: E111 or travel medical / evacuation (confirm coverage in US)
bike: Tool kit, tire repair, pump/CO2, spare tubes if applicable
Chain lube, zip ties, electrical tape, spare fuses
Phone mount + USB power, offline maps downloaded
ridingGear: Moisture-wicking base layers for humid Southeast / Mississippi valley legs (easier than cooking in cotton under armor)
camp: Tent / sleep system for high-altitude cold nights
Water capacity for UT/AZ/CA desert legs

emergency.notes: ICE: add contacts and any allergies. Yellowstone: nearest care may be far — carry first aid. If regional wildfire smoke drives AQI into unhealthy ranges, shorten the riding day and seek clean indoor air — especially at altitude or with any respiratory history.

beforeYouGo: Replace placeholder start/end dates in trip.json with your real departure window.

Maps: set GOOGLE_MAPS_API_KEY in .env (see .env.example), run npm run build, then serve. On Vercel, add the same variable under Environment Variables — build generates google-maps-config.js. Restrict the key by HTTP referrer (localhost + your Vercel URL). Re-check each leg in Maps after you set exact pins.
Primary stays stay first on each day; Bunk a Biker backups are in data/bab-hosts.json (edit babAlternateIds per day if you trim the list).
Confirm each host address, gate codes, and bike parking before arrival day.
Arches NP: check timed-entry reservation rules for your dates.
Yellowstone / Beartooth: verify pass opening and NPS alerts.

Every other national park or monument you actually enter on this loop (e.g. Zion tunnel / vehicle size rules, Smokies parking, timed shuttles, Navajo Nation tour permits): open NPS.gov or the managing tribe/agency site for **your** entry dates — rules and reservations change by season.

Surface hazards common on US Interstates and plains: grooved pavement, tar snakes, steel bridge decks, expansion joints, and cattle guards — scan ahead, reduce lean, and avoid hard throttle or braking on transitions.
Download offline maps for the full loop and share location with one trusted contact.
